findings.description: SQL injection vulnerability due to direct string concatenation of user input into SQL query.
findings.reasoning: (1) Data flow: username and password from req.query are directly concatenated into the SQL query string. (2) Trust boundary: username and password are external inputs from HTTP request. (3) Input validation: no sanitization or parameterized queries used. (4) Verdict: SQL injection vulnerability.
findings.remediation: Refer to OWASP Top-10 and NIST CWE guidance for this vulnerability class.

findings.description: SQL injection vulnerability due to unsanitized SQL query execution.
findings.reasoning: (1) Data flow: sql variable is directly used in db.query without sanitization or parameterized queries. (2) Trust boundary: sql can be influenced by external inputs if not properly handled. (3) Input validation: no evidence of input validation or prepared statements. (4) Verdict: SQL injection vulnerability.
findings.remediation: Refer to OWASP Top-10 and NIST CWE guidance for this vulnerability class.
